bullets.text: U.S. circulates an Iran peace proposal while increasing Middle East troop posture, signaling escalation risk alongside diplomacy.
Iran rejects direct U.S. talks but reviews proposals; crude prices rise on heightened Strait of Hormuz disruption risk.
Asia faces a double hit from higher oil and a stronger dollar, pressuring currencies, import costs, and growth outlook.
U.S. waives gasoline regulations and USPS seeks an 8% fuel surcharge as oil-driven inflation spills into consumer and shipping costs.
Mortgage rates near six-month highs cut demand; analysts warn the Iran war could further slow U.S. housing via rates, energy costs, and sentiment.
Freddie and Fannie bond buying is helping cap mortgage-rate increases; without it, borrowing costs would likely be materially higher.
Fed releases audited annual financial statements, a key transparency check on earnings remittances, balance-sheet operations, and risk management during quantitative tightening.
BEA reports 2025 U.S. international transactions and investment position, framing current-account flows and net foreign asset dynamics relevant to dollar and financing conditions.
Wall Street recession odds rise as data show underlying cracks; some strategists warn markets underprice downside risks.
Private credit’s low-loss narrative is weakening as defaults and investor exits increase, raising refinancing and systemic spillover concerns.
Jury finds Meta and YouTube negligent in a landmark social-media addiction case, increasing regulatory, liability, and product-design risk for major platforms.
Meta cuts several hundred jobs while pushing AI urgency, highlighting cost control and reallocation toward AI amid advertising and metaverse uncertainty.
White House says Trump will meet Xi in China in May, a major geopolitical signal for trade, tariffs, supply chains, and tech controls.
Trump criticizes Justices after Supreme Court tariff ruling, spotlighting legal constraints on executive trade powers and adding policy uncertainty for markets and importers.
Antimatter transported for the first time, enabling new precision tests of fundamental physics and advancing capabilities relevant to particle research and instrumentation.
